[[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Take-dera]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Take-dera]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[ja:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}}

Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}}

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}}

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}}

revision.comment: 73 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:竹寺 up to rev 102887760
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = {{ruby|竹寺|たけでら}} |画像 = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
本尊「牛頭天王」 |所在地 = 埼玉県飯能市大字南704番地 |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 53|緯度秒 = 24.3|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 11|経度秒 = 32.4|E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP-11 |山号 = [[醫王山]]{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}} |院号 = 薬寿院（[[薬樹院]]）{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}} |宗旨 = 天台宗{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}} |宗派 = |寺格 = |本尊 = [[牛頭天王]] |創建年 = [[天安 (日本)|天安]]元年（[[857年]]） |開基 = [[円仁]]（慈覚大師） |中興年 = |中興 = [[祥雲]]{{sfn|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}} |正式名 = 醫王山藥壽院八王寺 |別称 = |札所等 = [[武蔵野三十三観音霊場]]第三十三番
[[霊峰二山飯能七福神]]結願所 |文化財 = [[高野槙]] |公式HP = http://www.takedera.jp/ |公式HP名 = 飯能 竹寺 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=竹寺|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''竹寺'''（たけでら）は、[[埼玉県]][[飯能市]]{{small|大字}}[[南 (飯能市)|南]]{{small|字}}八王子[[秩父郡]]中澤組南村{{small|字}}八王子。[[吾野村]]南。吾野村は現在の飯能市大字[[坂石]]（阪石）、[[坂石町分]]（阪石町分）、南、[[南川 (飯能市)|南川]]、[[北川 (飯能市)|北川]]、[[高山 (飯能市)|高山]]、[[坂元 (飯能市)|坂元]]。にある[[天台宗]]の[[寺院]]である。正式名称は'''医王山薬寿院八王寺'''（いおうざんやくじゅいんはちおうじ）。[[神仏習合]]の寺として知られている。 [[武蔵野三十三観音霊場]]第三十三番札所。本尊は[[牛頭天王]]（[[本地仏]]は[[薬師如来]]）。 == 歴史 == [[天安 (日本)|天安]]元年（[[857年]]）に[[円仁]]（慈覚大師）が東国巡礼の際、病人が多いのを憐み、この地に道場を造り、大[[護摩]]の秘法を修したのが開山とされる。 本尊は牛頭天王、本地仏は薬師如来としているが[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]から免れ、神仏習合の寺となっている。 == 境内 == [[牛頭天王]]・本坊・三十三番結願堂・本地堂（瑠璃殿）・[[弁才天|弁天]]堂・[[稲荷神|稲荷]]社・水屋・奥の院・鳥居・石燈籠など。境内地{{Nowrap|11,768 [[平方メートル|m2]]}}。牛頭天王社の標高は{{Nowrap|490 [[メートル|m]]}}。登山で訪れる者も多い。本殿にあたる牛頭天王社は1999年焼失したが2003年に再建された{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=歴史と本尊|accessdate=2015-01-04}}。本殿内には木造牛頭天王坐像と八王子（牛頭天王の八人の童子）が祀られており、12年に一度、丑年に開帳される（開帳は2021年3月13日から同年12月12日まで）。 === 奥武蔵俳句寺 === 境内には、[[中谷孝雄]]や[[秋元不死男]]、[[松原地蔵尊]]などの句碑や絵馬が多くあり、秋元不死男が新聞紙上で「奥武蔵俳句寺」と評して以来、その異名がある。 === 茅の輪 === 登り口の鳥居に[[茅の輪]]が設けられている。また、木製の[[蘇民将来]]の護符を授与している。両者とも蘇民将来伝説に関係し、厄除けとされている。 {{gallery　|width= 180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|茅の輪 |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|本地堂（瑠璃殿） |Takedera Torii.jpg|竹寺の鳥居 }} == 文化財 == * 銅造[[大日如来]]坐像（[[室町時代]]末期） * [[十一面観音]]懸仏（[[永禄]]10年〔[[1567年]]〕） * 線刻[[勝軍地蔵]]懸仏（[[慶長]]16年〔[[1611年]]〕） * [[斎藤鶴磯]]筆「牛頭天王宮」額 * [[四神]]（[[白虎]]欠く） * [[高野槙]]（市指定[[文化財]]、天然記念物{{sfn|飯能市ホームページ}}） == 行事 == * 1月1日元旦祈願 * 竹あかり（桜編・星空編・紅葉編・除夜編） * 4月第一日曜日 写経会 * 5月5日 雨竹会 * 7月15日 牛頭天王大祭 * 10月第一日曜日 写経会 * 12月31日 除夜の鐘 == 付近の山、寺社 == * [[子ノ権現]][[天龍寺 (飯能市)|天龍寺]] – 徒歩約70分 * [[伊豆ヶ岳]]（標高{{Nowrap|850.9 [[メートル|m]]}}） * [[正丸峠]]（標高{{Nowrap|636 m}}） * [[大高山 (埼玉県)|大高山]]（標高{{Nowrap|493 m}}） * [[天覚山]]（標高{{Nowrap|445.5 m}}） == 関連項目 == * [[祇園信仰]] * [[八王子権現]] * [[薬師如来]] * [[修験道]] * [[陰陽道]] * [[解除会]]（[[茅の輪]]） ; [[霊峰二山飯能七福神]] # [[廣渡寺|{{small|平壽山}}廣渡寺]]（[[えびす|恵比寿]]尊天） – [[八幡町 (飯能市)|八幡町]]（[[真能寺]]）。曹洞宗。 # [[心應寺|{{small|萬壽山}}心應禪寺]]（[[弁財天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[原町 (飯能市)|原町]]（真能寺）{{small|字}}東関場。曹洞宗。 # [[瀧泉寺 (飯能市)|{{small|寶雲山觀音院}}瀧泉寺]]（[[寿老人]]） – [[日高市]][[横手 (日高市)|横手]]。[[真言宗智山派|智山派]]。 # [[長念寺 (飯能市)|{{small|清流山}}長念禪寺]]（[[毘沙門天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[白子 (飯能市)|白子]]。曹洞宗。 # [[興徳寺 (飯能市)|{{small|吉祥山}}興德寺]]（[[福禄寿]]尊） – {{small|大字}}[[井上 (飯能市)|井上]]（[[上井上村|上井上]]）。[[福徳寺 (飯能市)|楊秀山福德禪寺]]を兼務。[[臨済宗建長寺派|建長寺派]]。 # [[法光寺 (飯能市)|{{small|補陀山}}法光禪寺]]（[[布袋尊]]） – {{small|大字}}[[坂石町分]]。曹洞宗。 # [[秩父御嶽神社]]（[[大黒天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[坂石 (飯能市)|坂石]]{{small|字}}コモノ久保。[[御嶽山|御嶽権現]]・坂石村聖天社 # [[天龍寺 (飯能市)|子ノ権現{{small|大鱗山雲洞院}}天龍寺]]・子権現社（[[子ノ聖大権現]]） – {{small|大字}}南。天台宗特別寺 # 竹寺八王寺（[[牛頭天王]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ247秩父郡ノ2|chapter=中澤組 '''薬壽院'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=天然記念物|website=飯能市ホームページ|accessdate=2021-03-09|date=2019-04-08|ref={{sfnref|飯能市ホームページ}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp 武蔵野三十三観音霊場 公式ホームページ] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ 霊峰二山飯能七福神] {{武蔵野三十三観音霊場}} {{normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけてら}} [[Category:飯能市の寺]] [[Category:祇園信仰]] [[Category:薬師如来]] [[Category:観音菩薩]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]]

[[霊峰二山飯能七福神]] # [[廣渡寺|{{small|平壽山}}廣渡寺]]（[[えびす|恵比寿]]尊天） – [[八幡町 (飯能市)|八幡町]]（[[真能寺]]）。曹洞宗。 # [[心應寺|{{small|萬壽山}}心應禪寺]]（[[弁財天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[原町 (飯能市)|原町]]（真能寺）{{small|字}}東関場。曹洞宗。 # [[瀧泉寺 (飯能市)|{{small|寶雲山觀音院}}瀧泉寺]]（[[寿老人]]） – [[日高市]][[横手 (日高市)|横手]]。[[真言宗智山派|智山派]]。 # [[長念寺 (飯能市)|{{small|清流山}}長念禪寺]]（[[毘沙門天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[白子 (飯能市)|白子]]。曹洞宗。 # [[興徳寺 (飯能市)|{{small|吉祥山}}興德寺]]（[[福禄寿]]尊） – {{small|大字}}[[井上 (飯能市)|井上]]（[[上井上村|上井上]]）。[[福徳寺 (飯能市)|楊秀山福德禪寺]]を兼務。[[臨済宗建長寺派|建長寺派]]。 # [[法光寺 (飯能市)|{{small|補陀山}}法光禪寺]]（[[布袋尊]]） – {{small|大字}}[[坂石町分]]。曹洞宗。 # [[秩父御嶽神社]]（[[大黒天]]） – {{small|大字}}[[坂石 (飯能市)|坂石]]{{small|字}}コモノ久保。[[御嶽山|御嶽権現]]・坂石村聖天社 # [[天龍寺 (飯能市)|子ノ権現{{small|大鱗山雲洞院}}天龍寺]]・子権現社（[[子ノ聖大権現]]） – {{small|大字}}南。天台宗特別寺 # 竹寺八王寺（[[牛頭天王]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]|volume=巻ノ247秩父郡ノ2|chapter=中澤組 '''薬壽院'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=内務省地理局|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|新編武蔵風土記稿 中澤組}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=天然記念物|website=飯能市ホームページ|accessdate=2021-03-09|date=2019-04-08|ref={{sfnref|飯能市ホームページ}}}} == 外部リンク == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp 武蔵野三十三観音霊場 公式ホームページ] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ 霊峰二山飯能七福神] {{武蔵野三十三観音霊場}} {{normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけてら}} [[Category:飯能市の寺]] [[Category:祇園信仰]] [[Category:薬師如来]] [[Category:観音菩薩]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:天台宗の寺院]]

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Take-dera]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Draft categories| }} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]]

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takadera}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]]

Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|ja|常用漢字一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Yakushi Nyorai]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Nicknames]] [[Category:Tendai Sect Temples]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Take-dera]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]]
