[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]]

Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[en:draft:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[en:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion Beliefs]] [[Category:Kannon Bodhisattva]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Shinto-Buddhist Syncretism]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]

The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Take-dera]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Take-dera|Take-dera]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] [[temple]] located in [[Hannō City]], [[Saitama Prefecture]], in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the [[Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]]. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by [[:en:Ennin|Ennin]] (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric [[:en:goma|goma]] rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the [[Inari]] Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late [[Muromachi period]]) * Hanging statue of [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Faced Kannon]] (dated to [[:en:Eiroku|Eiroku]] 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to [[Keichō]] 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ([[Shirako]]). [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ([[Hotei]]) – Sakaishichōbun. [[Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * [[:en:List of jōyō kanji|List of jōyō kanji]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Temples in Hannō City]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Take-dera]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Take-dera]] [[sn:Take-dera]] [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:武蔵野三十三観音霊場]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]]
