Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[:en:Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] {{ill|Temple|en|Temple|WD=Q44539|lt=Temple|1=Temple}} located in [[Hannō City]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma|1=Goma}} rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the {{ill|Inari|en|Inari|WD=Q226639|lt=Inari|1=Inari}} Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}) * Hanging statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (dated to {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[:en:Shijin|Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[:en:Tenryūji|Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[:en:Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[:en:Yokote|Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ({{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako|1=Shirako}}). [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ({{ill|Hotei|en|Hotei|WD=Q63747792|lt=Hotei|1=Hotei}}) – Sakaishichōbun. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|WD=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Take-dera}} [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11599066}} [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma|1=Goma}} rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the {{ill|Inari|en|Inari|WD=Q226639|lt=Inari|1=Inari}} Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}) * Hanging statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (dated to {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[:en:Shijin|Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[:en:Tenryūji|Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[:en:Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[:en:Yokote|Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ({{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako|1=Shirako}}). [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ({{ill|Hotei|en|Hotei|WD=Q63747792|lt=Hotei|1=Hotei}}) – Sakaishichōbun. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|WD=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Take-dera}} [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11599066}} [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] [[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] [[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:竹寺.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}|prefecture={{ill|Hannō|WD=Q850472|1=Hannō}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.890083|N|139.192333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Ennin|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}|part_of={{ill|Musashino Sanjūsankannon|WD=Q24865854|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[:en:Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] {{ill|Temple|en|Temple|WD=Q44539|lt=Temple|1=Temple}} located in [[Hannō City]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma|1=Goma}} rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the {{ill|Inari|en|Inari|WD=Q226639|lt=Inari|1=Inari}} Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}) * Hanging statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (dated to {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[:en:Shijin|Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[:en:Tenryūji|Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[:en:Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[:en:Yokote|Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ({{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako|1=Shirako}}). [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|WD=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Take-dera}} [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11599066}} [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Everybody linked]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}) * Hanging statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (dated to {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[:en:Shijin|Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[:en:Tenryūji|Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[:en:Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[:en:Yokote|Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ({{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako|1=Shirako}}). [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ({{ill|Hotei|en|Hotei|WD=Q63747792|lt=Hotei|1=Hotei}}) – Sakaishichōbun. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|WD=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Take-dera}} [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11599066}} [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] [[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] [[ja:竹寺]][[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:竹寺.JPG|religious_affiliation={{ill|shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}|prefecture={{ill|Hannō|WD=Q850472|1=Hannō}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.890083|N|139.192333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Ennin|WD=Q732282|1=Ennin}}|deity={{ill|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}|part_of={{ill|Musashino Sanjūsankannon|WD=Q24865854|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}}} {{Japanese temple |Name = {{ruby|Takadera|Bamboo Temple}} |image = [[File:竹寺.JPG|240px]]
Main deity "Gozu Tenno" |Location = 704 Minami, Hannō City, Saitama Prefecture |Latitude degrees = 35|Latitude minutes = 53|Latitude seconds = 24.3|N/S = N |Longitude degrees = 139|Longitude minutes = 11|Longitude seconds = 32.4|E/W = E |Map country code = JP-11 |Mountain name = [[Iōzan]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Temple name = Yakujūin (also known as Yakujūin){{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Denomination = Tendai Buddhism{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Main deity = [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] |Year of foundation = [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] 1 (857) |Founder = {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) |Revival year = |Revival figure = [[Shōun]]{{sfn|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}} |Official name = Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji |Other names = |Pilgrimage designation = 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}
Final stop of the [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] |Cultural properties = [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine) |Official website = http://www.takedera.jp/ |Official website name = Hannō Takadera |Map 2 = {{Location map|Japan Saitama Prefecture|label=Takadera|float=center|relief=1|width=240}} }} '''Takadera''' (Bamboo Temple) is a [[:en:Tendai Buddhism|Tendai sect]] {{ill|Temple|en|Temple|WD=Q44539|lt=Temple|1=Temple}} located in [[Hannō City]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the area of Minami, Hachioji.Formerly part of [[Chichibu District]]'s Chūzawa village, later integrated into Hannō City. Minami corresponds to areas now known as Sakaishi, Sakaishichōbun, and others. Its official name is '''Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji''' (Iōzan Yakujūin Hachioji). The temple is known for its unique combination of [[Shinto-Buddhist syncretism]]. It is the 33rd station of the {{ill|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|en|Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|WD=Q24865854|lt=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage|1=Musashino Kannon Pilgrimage}}. The principal deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], with [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] as the associated deity (*honjibutsu*). == History == The temple was founded in the first year of [[Ten'an (Japan)|Ten'an]] (857) by {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|WD=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) during his pilgrimage in the eastern provinces. He was moved by the suffering of many sick people and established a sanctuary at this site to perform esoteric {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma|1=Goma}} rituals. While the temple's main deity is [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]], the associated deity is [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]]. Despite the [[Shinbutsu bunri]] (separation of Shinto and Buddhism) policies during the [[Meiji Restoration]], the temple retained its syncretic practices. == Grounds == The temple grounds include structures such as the Gozu Tenno Shrine, the main hall, the 33rd station hall, the Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*), the Benten Hall, the {{ill|Inari|en|Inari|WD=Q226639|lt=Inari|1=Inari}} Shrine, water facilities, the Okunoin (inner sanctuary), a torii gate, stone lanterns, and more. The grounds cover an area of {{nowrap|11,768 square meters}}. The Gozu Tenno Shrine, situated at an elevation of {{nowrap|490 meters}}, attracts many visitors, including hikers. The shrine building, which burned down in 1999, was rebuilt in 2003.{{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.takedera.com/link.html|title=History and Main Deity|accessdate=2015-01-04}} The shrine houses a wooden seated statue of Gozu Tenno and eight child deities (*Hachioji*), which are displayed only once every 12 years, during the Year of the Ox (next opening: March 13 to December 12, 2021). === Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple === The temple grounds contain numerous haiku monuments and ema (votive tablets) dedicated to poets like [[Takao Nakaya]] and [[Fumio Akimoto]]. Since Akimoto referred to it as the "Oku-Musashi Haiku Temple" in a newspaper, the temple has been known by this nickname. === Chigaya Ring === At the entrance torii, a [[chigaya ring]] (*chinowa*) is set up, and visitors can obtain wooden amulets of [[:en:Somin Shorai|Somin Shorai]]. Both are connected to the legend of Somin Shorai and are considered protective charms. {{gallery|width=180 |Kayanowa, Takedera.jpg|Chigaya Ring |Ruriden, Takedera.jpg|Ruriden (*Hall of the Honjibutsu*) |Takedera Torii.jpg|Torii Gate of Takadera }} == Cultural Properties == * Bronze seated statue of [[:en:Dainichi Nyorai|Dainichi Nyorai]] (late {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}) * Hanging statue of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (dated to {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|WD=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 10, 1567) * Line-engraved hanging statue of [[Shōgun Jizō]] (dated to {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} 16, 1611) * Gaku (plaque) inscribed "Gozu Tenno Shrine" by [[Kakusei Tsurusai]] * [[:en:Shijin|Shijin]] (*Four Gods*, missing White Tiger) * [[Kōyamaki]] (Japanese Umbrella Pine), designated as a city cultural property and natural monument{{sfn|Hannō City Official Website}} == Events == * January 1: New Year's Prayer * Bamboo Light Festival (Spring, Starry Night, Autumn, New Year's Eve Editions) * First Sunday of April: Sutra Copying Gathering * May 5: Rain Bamboo Festival * July 15: Gozu Tenno Grand Festival * First Sunday of October: Sutra Copying Gathering * December 31: New Year's Eve Bell Ringing == Nearby Mountains and Temples == * [[Nenokongen Temple]] ([[:en:Tenryūji|Tenryūji]]) – About a 70-minute walk * [[Mount Izuga]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|850.9 meters}}) * [[Shōmaru Pass]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|636 meters}}) * [[Ōtakayama]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|493 meters}}) * [[Tengaku Mountain]] (Elevation: {{nowrap|445.5 meters}}) == See Also == * [[Gion Faith]] * [[Hachiōji Gongen|Hachioji Gongen]] * [[:en:Yakushi Nyorai|Yakushi Nyorai]] * [[Shugendō]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Kaijo-e]] (Chigaya Ring Ritual) ; [[Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods]] # [[Kōdōji|{{small|Heijuzan}} Kōdōji]] ([[Ebisu]]) – [[Hachiman Town]], [[Shinōji]]. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Shinōji|{{small|Manjuzan}} Shinōji]] ([[Benzaiten]]) – Minami Town, east area. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[:en:Ryūsenji|{{small|Hōunzan Kannonin}} Ryūsenji]] ([[Jurōjin|Jurojin]]) – [[Hidaka City]] ([[:en:Yokote|Yokote]]). [[Shingon Chizan Sect]]. # [[Chōnenji|{{small|Seiryuzan}} Chōnenji]] ([[:en:Bishamonten|Bishamonten]]) – Minami Town ({{ill|Shirako|en|Shirako|WD=Q1203293|lt=Shirako|1=Shirako}}). [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Kōtokuji|{{small|Kisshōzan}} Kōtokuji]] ([[Fukurokuju]]) – [[Inoue Town]] ([[Kami-Inoue Village]]). [[Rinzai Kenchōji Sect]]. # [[Hōkōji|{{small|Fudazan}} Hōkōji]] ({{ill|Hotei|en|Hotei|WD=Q63747792|lt=Hotei|1=Hotei}}) – Sakaishichōbun. [[:en:Sōtō Zen|Sōtō Zen]]. # [[Chichibu Mitake Shrine]] ([[Daikokuten]]) – [[Sakaishi Town]]. [[Mitake Shrine]]. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{cite book|Japanese book|title=[[New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript]]|volume=Volume 247, Chichibu District 2|chapter=Chūzawa Section '''Yakujūin'''|id={{NDLJP|764013/24}}|publisher=Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications Geography Bureau|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|New Edition Musashi Fudoki Manuscript Chūzawa Section}}}} * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/article/detail/599|title=Natural Monuments|website=Hannō City Official Website|accessdate=2021-3-9|date=2019-4-08|ref={{sfnref|Hannō City Official Website}}}} == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [https://63433.jp Official Website of the Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage] * [https://reihou2-hanno7.com/ Reihō Nizan Hannō Seven Lucky Gods] {{Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage}} {{normdaten}} {{wiktionary}} ==See Also== * {{ill|List of jōyō kanji|en|List of jōyō kanji|WD=Q841128|lt=List of jōyō kanji|1=List of jōyō kanji}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Gion cult}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|竹寺|version=102887760|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Take-dera}} [[ja:竹寺]] [[fr:Take-dera]] [[zh:Take-dera]] [[ja:Take-dera]] [[zh:竹寺 (日本)]] {{wikidata link|Q11599066}} [[Category:Avalokiteśvara]] [[Category:Bhaiṣajyaguru]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Musashino 33 Kannon Pilgrimage]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Temples in Hanno, Saitama]] [[Category:Tiantai temples]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
